Description: The Blue Bird Café fire was a nightclub tragedy in Montreal, resulting in 37 deaths due to arson on the staircase that acted as the main entrance/exit for the Wagon Wheel bar patrons.

Building Description: Large, windowless structure with breeze block walls, corrugated iron roof, grottos made of polyurethane and papier-mâché, flammable decorations on pillars
Aftermath: Revealed shortcomings in fire safety, shock to the French nation, new safety measures implemented
Causes: Carelessly discarded match igniting a foam-filled seat cushion
Criminal Charges: Brought against some individuals, with some receiving suspended jail sentences

Aftermath: Welansky held responsible, advancements in treatment of burn victims, blood banks, hospitals preparedness
Safety Violations: Locked exit doors, flammable decorations, wartime shortage of freon in air-conditioning
Causes: Electrical short, methyl chloride in air conditioning unit
Historical Significance: Second-deadliest single-building fire in U.S. history, led to new fire-safety laws for public establishments

Description: Two unlicensed bars (The Spanish Rooms and Rodo's/El Dandy), patrons primarily Spanish and Latin American, closed-off access, police awareness, fueling incident involving overcharging and perpetrator John Thompson

Cause: Ignition of corn starch powder resulting in a dust fire
Description: Staff sprayed corn starch powder which ignited, leading to a dust fire that burned multiple attendees. The incident was initially misunderstood as an explosion due to the dispersal of the burning powder.

Location: 1959 Southern Boulevard, West Farms section of the Bronx in New York City, United States
Safety Violations: Lack of fire exits, alarms, sprinkler system
Historical Significance: Deadliest fire in New York City since Triangle Shirtwaist Factory fire in 1911; deadliest in American territory since Dupont Plaza Hotel fire in Puerto Rico in 1986

Cause of Fire: Sparks in disco booth, electrical malfunction
Aftermath: Worst fire in Philippine history, significant casualties, legal repercussions
Safety Violations: Blocked emergency exit, exit locked by guards

Cause of Fire: Electrical fault in non-planning-permission-compliant storage room with flammable materials
Building Description: Former food factory converted into a nightclub with dance floor, stage, bars, and seating areas
Background: Converted from a food factory in 1948 by the Butterly family
Fire Outbreak: Small fire observed initially, believed to have originated from an electrical fault

Cause of Fire: Started by three boys smoking in a disused kiosk with a carelessly discarded match or stub
Building Description: 3.5 acres, 50,000 sq ft, 5 floors, cost of £2 million, designed for 10,000 tourists

Significance: Deadliest known attack on a gay club in U.S. history until 2016 Orlando nightclub shooting

Location: Whiskey Au Go Go, Brisbane, Australia
Warnings Issued: Threats made and verified, arson at empty club
Fire Start Details: Ignition of two petrol drums in the building's foyer, poor escape route
